title: banksia rufa subsp. obliquiloba
intro: Banksia rufa subsp. obliquiloba is a subspecies of shrub native to Western Australia, characterized by its distinctive, serrated leaves and conical inflorescences. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which are attributed to the presence of terpenoids and flavonoids in its leaves. The health benefits of Banksia rufa subsp. obliquiloba include its potential to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its role in supporting immune function. The plant is often harvested for its leaves and inflorescences, which are used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety as a medicinal agent.
